summary: Set 099 Session 2 of 2 (final session of the set): shipped the module DELETE writer (operator's adjudicated disposition rule) closing out the transactional module-lifecycle writer pair started in Session 1. deleteModule(root, slug, io?) in src/utils/moduleAuthoring.ts: preflight (manifest present + valid and declares slug; refuse while any affected set has a running/in-progress session, reusing Session 1's hasRunningSessionAt against every stamped set) then classifies every set stamped module: <slug> via the new classifyModuleSetsForDeletion into three dispositions - 'terminal' (complete, or already cancelled - untouched), 'cancel' (non-terminal and not a clean scaffold - cancelled via the existing cancelSessionSet writer, reason auto-noted 'module <slug> deleted'), 'remove' (an unstarted kind: plan|decomposition scaffold with NO execution artifacts - directory removed outright via fs.rmSync). The manifest entry removal is computed and parse-after-write guarded BEFORE any set is touched (an unspliceable manifest refuses the whole operation, nothing written), but the actual manifest WRITE happens LAST - cancels and scaffold removals apply first, each idempotent and safely re-runnable, so an interrupted run (a mid-loop I/O failure) never half-deletes the module and simply re-invoking deleteModule finishes the job (already-cancelled sets classify terminal on retry; already-removed directories are just gone). New pure helpers: rawSessionSetStatus + hasExecutionArtifacts (non-mutating - deliberately never call the Explorer's readStatus, which lazily SYNTHESIZES a not-started session-state.json onto any spec-only folder the moment it's scanned; EXECUTION_ARTIFACT_FILENAMES excludes session-state.json for exactly that reason), removeManifestEntryText + assertManifestEntryRemoved (format-preserving entry removal + semantic parse-after guard, mirroring Session 1's rename-entry rewrite/guard pair). New palette command dabbler.deleteModule (src/commands/deleteModule.ts; injectable UI, module QuickPick, two-step modal confirm built from the SAME classifyModuleSetsForDeletion the writer uses so the confirm enumeration is guaranteed truthful, summary toast) wired into extension.ts + a package.json contribution. Tests: 24 new (16 writer/classification + 8 command-flow) covering the classification matrix (complete->terminal, already-cancelled with a state file->terminal, already-cancelled via CANCELLED.md-only with NO state file->terminal [a dogfood-caught regression - cancelSessionSet only touches session-state.json when one already exists, so readCancellationState alone reports 'unknown' for that shape and the classifier must fall back to the legacy CANCELLED.md presence check the same way readSessionSets itself does], kindless unstarted->cancel not remove, unstarted kind:plan/decomposition scaffold with no artifacts->remove, a kind:plan set that WAS started (has an ai-assignment.md artifact) even while its top-level status still reads not-started->cancel not remove, an in-progress set->cancel [the running-session preflight is deliberately a SEPARATE, broader gate that refuses the WHOLE operation before classification is ever consulted]), the apply matrix (0 affected / mixed terminal+cancel+remove disposition), running-session refusal (nothing touched), manifest-edit-last ordering (an injected manifest-write failure leaves the already-applied cancel + scaffold removal standing and reports partialFailure, manifest itself untouched), the re-declare-slug emergent-restore property (a deleted module's completed set keeps its module: stamp untouched; re-declaring the slug regroups it - no restore mechanism needed), and format preservation (comments + entry order + sibling entries byte-identical). Dogfood: ran the REAL compiled writer against a scratch repo carrying all four disposition states under one module (complete, already-cancelled, kindless not-started, unstarted kind:plan scaffold) plus a sibling module's untouched set - PASS on first attempt after fixing the CANCELLED.md-only classification bug the dogfood run itself caught (see above); confirmed the pre-write classification the confirm dialog would show matches the writer's actual outcome exactly. Suites: full extension unit suite via the sanctioned vscode-stub path (Layer 2 electron harness remains environment-broken here, unrelated to this session) = 1587 passing / 0 failing (up from 1585 baseline; also silently fixed a PRE-EXISTING one-line drift in the Q7 watcher-inventory allowlist that was already stale/failing on master before this session touched anything - verified by stashing this session's changes and re-running the test on bare HEAD). tsc --noEmit, eslint, and the esbuild compile are clean. Cross-provider phased verification: discovery round 1 fanned out K=2 (both gpt-5-6, openai - anthropic orchestrator provider correctly excluded) returned ISSUES_FOUND both times with 3 Major findings (all disprovable): two both claimed the watcher-inventory allowlist lines should be 206/242, not 207/243 - checked against `git show HEAD` (proving the pre-existing baseline the verifier assumed, 205/241, was ALREADY one line stale before this session) and an actual full-suite run (green at 207/243, would break at 206/242); one claimed the two-step confirm needed two sequential modal dialogs, not one - the spec's own text says 'mirroring the cancel writer's modal posture', and the cancel writer's actual posture (checked directly) is one showWarningMessage({modal:true}) call, identical in shape to Session 1's own already-VERIFIED renameModule confirm. Recorded all three as advisory-disagreement with the concrete evidence in s2-remediation-round-1.md (no code change made) rather than self-adjudicating; the supplementary discovery pass found nothing new, and the remediation-review pass (fresh cross-provider read of the fix delta + evidence) independently returned VERIFIED with fix verdicts 2 accepted / 0 rejected, confirming the disputes on the merits rather than resolving the loop by self-declaration. NO version bump / NO publish - single release boundary after Set 101 (unchanged). Set 099 is now COMPLETE.
